algorithms.title: History-Taking and Secondary Assessment Sequence
algorithms.description: Gathering history and performing a developmentally appropriate exam after the primary assessment.
steps: Obtain SAMPLE history, asking the child directly when developmentally appropriate and using the caregiver to supplement.
Add pediatric-specific elements: birth/developmental history (infants), immunizations, growth/weight trends.
Choose exam order by development: toe-to-head for infants/toddlers; head-to-toe for school-age/adolescents.
Perform a focused exam (stable/low-priority) or rapid exam (high-priority, often en route).
Apply family-centered care: keep a calm, cooperative caregiver present and involved where feasible.
Adapt communication for special needs: professional interpretation, hearing-impaired accommodations, developmental-difference considerations.
Integrate findings with the PAT and primary assessment to finalize the treatment and transport plan.
